steps.title: Find the slope of the radius to the tangent point
steps.content:
The radius connects the center $(-1, 1)$ to the tangent point $(5, -4)$:
$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-4 - 1}{5 - (-1)} = \frac{-5}{6}$$

steps.title: Get the tangent line's slope and equation
steps.content:
A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency, so the tangent's slope is the negative reciprocal:
$$m_{\text{tangent}} = \frac{6}{5}$$
Using point-slope form through $(5, -4)$:
$$y + 4 = \frac{6}{5}(x - 5) \;\Rightarrow\; y = \frac{6}{5}x - 10$$

steps.title: Test each choice in $y = \frac{6}{5}x - 10$
steps.content:
• A) $\left(0, rac{6}{5} ight)$: $ rac{6}{5}(0) - 10 = -10 e rac{6}{5}$. Eliminate.
  • B) $(4, 7)$: $ rac{6}{5}(4) - 10 = rac{24}{5} - 10 = - rac{26}{5} e 7$. Eliminate.
  • C) $(10, 2)$: $ rac{6}{5}(10) - 10 = 12 - 10 = 2$. Match.
  • D) $(11, 1)$: $ rac{6}{5}(11) - 10 = rac{66}{5} - 10 = rac{16}{5} e 1$. Eliminate.
That confirms C is correct.
